claim: Normal expression of RUNX1 has tumor-promoting effects.

result.claim: Normal expression of RUNX1 has tumor-promoting effects.
result.reasoning: The evidence strongly refutes the claim that normal expression of RUNX1 has tumor-promoting effects. Paper ID 1982286, "Reverse engineering of TLX oncogenic transcriptional networks identifies RUNX1 as tumor suppressor in T-ALL," explicitly states that RUNX1 was predicted to have a tumor-suppressor role in T cell transformation (Proposition ID: prop_1451) and identifies RUNX1 as a tumor-suppressor gene in T-ALL. Furthermore, the paper notes that recurrent somatic loss-of-function mutations in RUNX1 were identified in human T-ALL (Proposition ID: prop_1452), which is consistent with a tumor-suppressor function, as loss of function in such genes contributes to cancer development. No evidence was found to support a tumor-promoting role for normal RUNX1 expression.
